title: Quantile regression to examine the association of air pollution with subclinical atherosclerosis in an adolescent population.
summary: Cross-sectional study using quantile regression to examine the association between ambient air pollution (NO2, PM2.5, PMcoarse, ultrafine particles) and carotid intima-media thickness (CIMT) in a Dutch adolescent birth cohort.
key_findings: The study aims to explore if associations occur at different percentiles of the CIMT distribution.
population: 363 adolescents (age 16) in the Dutch PIAMA birth cohort
